subject: Geography
title: Why Cities Grow
description: A short lesson to help you apply Why Cities Grow.

hook.text: Cities grow because they concentrate jobs, services, and opportunity in one place.

buildup.text: Factories, ports, and trade routes historically attracted workers who built neighborhoods.

discovery.text: Today, the knowledge economy pulls educated workers into tech hubs and financial centers.

twist.text: Poverty also drives growth. Millions move to cities not for opportunity but out of desperation.

climax.text: Every megacity is a magnet—pulling people in with promises it can't always keep.

punchline.text: Cities grow because people believe they'll find something better.

quiz.question: What is the primary reason cities grow?
options: Government mandates force people to move
Climate is always better in cities
